response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack software engineer with roughly 15 years of experience across web, mobile, and some systems programming, but the role demands principal-level hardware performance architecture expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears anywhere in his background. His closest relevant work (low-latency DLang microservices, brief C/C++ IDE component development at National Instruments) is software-level performance engineering, not silicon or accelerator architecture, leaving a fundamental domain gap that seniority in adjacent fields does not bridge.
strengths: Extensive experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems (DLang async I/O services handling millions of requests per second)
Broad engineering versatility across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with strong testing and software-craftsmanship practices
Meets the formal education-plus-experience bar (BS in Computer Science with well over 8 years of technical experience)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, chip architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience whatsoever
No deep-learning workload, AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, misaligned with a principal hardware performance architect track

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL workload or GPU optimization experience
